days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant streets

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: After your flight, follow the signs to the Airport Express train station for a quick 24-minute ride into the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel
timeline.detail: Settle into the Hotel ICON, a stylish hotel located a short walk from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station, perfect for exploring.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Savor local dim sum at this popular eatery; don't miss their signature shrimp dumplings. Arrive early to avoid the crowd.
timeline.mapQuery: DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Explore the rich history of Hong Kong through engaging exhibits that detail its cultural heritage from prehistoric times to present day.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Stroll along Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Enjoy breathtaking views of Victoria Harbour and the skyline. Perfect time for photos at sunset; try capturing the iconic skyline reflection.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Hutong
timeline.detail: Dine on Northern Chinese cuisine with stunning views of the city. Be sure to try the Peking Duck — a must-try when in Hong Kong!
timeline.mapQuery: Hutong, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card at the airport for convenient travel on public transport.
Dim sum restaurants can be very crowded; consider going early.
Check the free admission days for museums to save on costs.

days.title: Exploring Central and Mong Kok
days.subtitle: Food and culture immersion

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day at this famous Hong Kong-style café. Enjoy a hearty breakfast set featuring toast, macaroni, and milk tea.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Man Mo Temple
timeline.detail: Wander around this historic temple dedicated to the gods of literature and war. Admire its beautiful architecture and atmospheric incense smoke.
timeline.mapQuery: Man Mo Temple, Sheung Wan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Savor authentic wonton noodles at one of the best noodle shops in Hong Kong. Their wontons are made fresh daily.

timeline.title: Shopping in Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant streets of Mong Kok, known for its markets and boutiques. Don't miss the Ladies' Market for souvenirs.

timeline.title: Street Food Dinner at Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Experience Hong Kong's street food scene; try egg waffles, stinky tofu, and fish balls while strolling through Mong Kok streets.

timeline.title: Explore the Night Market
timeline.detail: Visit the Temple Street Night Market filled with stalls selling everything from clothing to fortune-telling services, ideal for local shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Jordan, Hong Kong

localTips: Visit at least one street food stall in Mong Kok for an authentic experience.
Bring cash for street food and small shops in markets.
Explore different alleys in Mong Kok; you might find hidden gems.

days.title: Nature Day in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Discovering the great outdoors

timeline.title: Breakfast at Café de Coral
timeline.detail: Enjoy a local breakfast with Hong Kong-style milk tea and a variety of breakfast sets at this popular café chain.
timeline.mapQuery: Café de Coral, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Hike on Lion Rock
timeline.detail: Embark on a scenic hike up Lion Rock for stunning views of the city and countryside. The trail is moderately challenging but rewarding.

timeline.title: Lunch at a Local Cha Chaan Teng
timeline.detail: Refuel after your hike with lunch at a nearby Cha Chaan Teng, sampling local favorites like baked rice dishes or instant noodles.
timeline.mapQuery: Cha Chaan Teng, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Nan Lian Garden
timeline.detail: Relax in the peaceful atmosphere of Nan Lian Garden, showcasing beautiful landscapes and traditional Chinese architecture.
timeline.mapQuery: Nan Lian Garden, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to the Hotel to Refresh
timeline.detail: Head back to the hotel for a quick shower and relax before dinner. Enjoy the stunning views from your room.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Farewell Dinner at Aqua
timeline.detail: Enjoy an upscale dining experience with panoramic views of the harbour and skyline, perfect for a final meal in the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Aqua, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Take plenty of water for the hike; it can get hot!
Visit early in the morning to avoid crowds at Nan Lian Garden.
Ask the staff at your hotel for transportation options back to the airport.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Modern hotel with stunning harbour views, 5-min walk from TST MTR.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong
